user_title: COO & Global Head of Change Execution, WPS

talking_points.headline: HSBC Q1 $9.4bn Profit, 17.3% RoTE — WPS Must Scale Operating Backbone for H2
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's strong Q1 results — driven by 49% insurance income growth and 21% investment distribution rise — create immediate WPS capacity requirements that must be operational before H2 peak season to sustain the momentum.
bullets: HSBC posted $9.4bn Q1 pre-tax profit (RoTE 17.3%) with revenue +4%; wealth fees rose 20% YoY, insurance income +49%, and investment distribution revenues +21% — all scaling processing, distribution and compliance demands on WPS operational infrastructure.

The group attracted $39bn NNIA in Q1 with $34bn from Asia — WPS must validate that capacity headroom exists for H2 seasonal peaks and the Wealth Powerhouse integration, which consolidates previously parallel governance tracks into a single operating model.
Operating expenses grew only 5% against revenue growth — the cost efficiency ratio Surali's business management mandate must protect as wealth-driven volumes accelerate through the second half.

source_links.title: WealthBriefing: HSBC IWPB Q1 Revenue Detail

talking_points.headline: APAC Regulators Move to Active Enforcement — WPS AI and IT Governance Must Be Audit-Ready
talking_points.why_it_matters: Deloitte's Q1 update and Bloomberg's outlook both confirm regulators across Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan, and Indonesia are moving from publishing frameworks to active supervision — the governance gap Surali must close before H2 examination cycles.
bullets: Deloitte's Q1 2026 APAC update confirms governance and accountability as the #1 regulatory theme; Japan, Indonesia, and Singapore all issued new bank IT governance requirements in Q1 — WPS must map these to HSBC's existing control frameworks and identify gaps.

MAS is consulting on comprehensive AI Risk Management Guidelines covering explainability, lifecycle controls, and oversight of third-party AI service providers — WPS must engage in the consultation process and build audit-ready documentation before guidelines are finalised.

Bloomberg's APAC regulatory outlook explicitly confirms supervisory agendas are moving from 'framework setting' to 'new guidance, implementation, and enforcement' — WPS governance must be ahead of the cycle, not reactive.

talking_points.headline: Lloyds £100M AI Target Sets the Benchmark — WPS Must Define HSBC's Equivalent ROI Framework
talking_points.why_it_matters: Lloyds' explicit and measurable AI value targets give Surali a directly comparable peer benchmark for WPS governance discussions about HSBC's enterprise AI programme scale, return expectations, and accountability ownership.
bullets: Lloyds Banking Group delivered £50m in measured AI value in 2025 and targets £100m in 2026 via its Envoy platform — deployed across 21m accounts, spanning customer, operations, risk, and engineering. This is a governance model WPS can reference directly.

OCBC's Q1 2026 data (wealth income +50% YoY, AUM record S$342bn) reflects the compounding effect of sustained technology investment and disciplined operational execution — the model WPS change governance should be benchmarking against in HSBC's H2 planning.

With only 35% of wealth advisors industry-wide using AI regularly, the WPS governance question is: does HSBC have a defined AI adoption rate target, measurement framework, and clear accountability ownership sitting within WPS scope?

source_links.title: FinTech Mag: Lloyds Agentic AI 21M Accounts
Fintech Global: AI Implementation Gap Analysis

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 IWPB: Wealth Fees +15% YoY, Revenue Reaches $3.75bn
HSBC News.summary: HSBC's International Wealth and Premier Banking revenues rose 1.5% YoY to $3.75bn in Q1 2026. Wealth fees and other income grew 15% driven by insurance (+19%), investment distribution (+21%), and private banking (+8%). Pre-tax profit held stable at $1.23bn while operating expenses grew 5%.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Q1 2026 Group Results: $9.4bn Profit, 17.3% RoTE, $39bn NNIA
HSBC News.summary: HSBC posted $9.4bn group pre-tax profit for Q1 2026 with a 17.3% RoTE. Revenue rose 4% driven by wealth fees (+20%) and FX income. Insurance income grew 49%, investment distribution revenues rose 21%. The group attracted $39bn in net new money in Q1, of which $34bn came from Asia, reinforcing the Asia-wealth pivot.

AI & Technology.title: AI in Wealth Management: Closing the 65% Implementation Gap
AI & Technology.summary: Only 35% of financial advisory intermediaries actively use AI tools, with just 10.5% doing so daily. The analysis identifies data governance, talent, and change management as the binding constraints. Banks that systematize AI into advice workflows achieve repeatable, auditable, scalable advisory at lower cost — the decisive differentiator in 2026 as competitors accelerate deployment.

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Q1 2026: Wealth AUM Hits Record S$342bn, Fee Income +34% YoY
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC's Q1 2026 results show wealth management AUM reaching a record S$342bn (US$271bn), up 12% YoY, with fee income rising 34% to S$422m. Group net profit grew 5% to S$1.97bn. OCBC continues to integrate HSBC's Indonesia wealth business (1,300 staff, S$6.6bn AUM) while hiring 30-50 wealth bankers in Hong Kong under its Next Frontier strategy.

Competitor Intelligence.title: OCBC Q1 2026: Net Profit +5%, Wealth Income +50%, Next Frontier Executing
Competitor Intelligence.summary: OCBC reported Q1 2026 net profit of S$1.97bn (+5% YoY) with wealth management income rising 50% from a year earlier. Corporate wealth income more than tripled. OCBC is executing its Next Frontier strategy, strengthening its Singapore and Hong Kong hubs while completing its S$2.5bn capital return plan and integrating the HSBC Indonesia wealth acquisition.

Regulatory & Markets.title: Deloitte APAC Q1 2026 Regulatory Update: Governance Top Priority
Regulatory & Markets.summary: Deloitte's Q1 2026 APAC regulatory update identifies governance and accountability as the dominant theme. Key developments include Japan finalizing cyber risk preparedness guidelines, Indonesia mandating IT governance for banks, MAS consulting on comprehensive AI Risk Management Guidelines (covering explainability and lifecycle controls), and Hong Kong launching Phase 2A of its Sustainable Finance Taxonomy.
